codeReview.summary: Implementation successfully creates lectures.json with content for Lectures 1 and 2, and updates LectureReader to load from JSON. All verification steps passed - lectures display with proper content, syntax highlighting, and section navigation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Commit d3161da contains unrelated changes from multiple missions (ui-fixes mission, scrutiny reviews, content-extraction) in violation of AGENTS.md small PRs guidance
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show d3161da --stat shows: .factory/services.yaml, .factory/skills/frontend-worker/SKILL.md, scrutiny reviews, AND data/processed/lectures.json - all in one commit

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature skillName is 'frontend-worker' but the skill doesn't exist in the skills registry. Worker proceeded manually based on AGENTS.md
sharedStateObservations.evidence: skillFeedback.deviations: skillName 'frontend-worker' was not found, proceeded manually

sharedStateObservations.observation: validation-contract.md lacks semantic content assertions (VAL-CONTENT-*) - only structural validation is performed. Worker extracted content from PDFs but there's no way to verify the content semantically matches the feature description
sharedStateObservations.evidence: VAL-LECT-001, VAL-LECT-002, VAL-LECT-003 only verify 'lecture exists', 'has sections', 'code blocks' - not that content matches feature description (e.g., Lecture 1 should cover binary/hex numbering, Lecture 2 should cover registers MOV)

summary: Feature extract-lecture-1-2 PASSES review. Worker successfully extracted content for Lectures 1 and 2 into lectures.json and updated LectureReader component. Build passes, interactive verification confirmed lectures render correctly with syntax highlighting and section navigation. Non-blocking issues: commit contains unrelated changes (violates small PRs guidance), skillName doesn't exist (worker proceeded manually), and validation-contract lacks semantic content assertions.
